revision.text: #redirect[[en:Yodo Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''與杼神社'''（よどじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[伏見区]]淀にある神社。 祭神は、[[豊玉姫]]命、[[高皇産霊神]]、[[速秋津姫]]命の三柱。
==歴史== [[愛宕念仏寺]]などを再興した[[千観]][[内供]]が[[応和]]年間(961年-963年)頃に[[肥前国]]（現在の佐賀県）[[佐賀郡]][[河上村]]の[[與止日女神社]]より[[淀大明神]]を勧請して建立。
==建造物== ===文化財建造物=== *拝殿 重要文化財
===かつて存在した建築物=== *宝塔 元禄年間（1688年-1704年）頃に建立（または再建）された多宝塔。明治初年の神仏分離で破却されたという。 *本殿 昭和46年（1971年）に重要文化財に指定されたが昭和50年（1975年）8月5日に花火の火による火災で焼失した。昭和55年（1979年）現在の本殿が再建された。
==参考資料== *黒川道祐「雍州府志」（1686年） *「都名所圖會」（1780年）
==外部リンク== *[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/yuisyo.html 公式サイト] *[http://www.d1.dion.ne.jp/~s_minaga/sos_yodojinzya.htm 與杼神社多宝塔]
{{DEFAULTSORT:よとしんしや}}
[[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:伏見区]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:現存しない日本の建築物]]

'''與杼神社'''（よどじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[伏見区]]淀にある神社。 祭神は、[[豊玉姫]]命、[[高皇産霊神]]、[[速秋津姫]]命の三柱。

==建造物== ===文化財建造物=== *拝殿 1649年頃建立の江戸時代建築で国の重要文化財に指定されている。
===かつて存在した建築物=== *宝塔 元禄年間（1688年-1704年）頃に建立（または再建）された多宝塔。明治初年の神仏分離で破却されたという。 *本殿 1649年頃の建立で昭和46年（1971年）に重要文化財に指定されたが昭和50年（1975年）8月5日に花火の火による火災で焼失した。昭和55年（1980年）現在の本殿が再建された。
==参考資料== *黒川道祐「雍州府志」（1686年） *「都名所圖會」（1780年）
==外部リンク== *[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/yuisyo.html 公式サイト] *[http://www.d1.dion.ne.jp/~s_minaga/sos_yodojinzya.htm 與杼神社多宝塔]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 與杼神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Yodojinja01.jpg|thumb|與杼神社正面|250px]] |所在地 = [[京都市]][[伏見区]]淀本町167 |ISO = JP-26 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 19.2 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 04.3 |ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[豊玉姫]]命、[[高皇産霊神]]、[[速秋津姫]]命の三柱） |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = [[応和]]年間（[[961年]]-[[964年]]） |例祭 = 元旦祭(1月1日)、節分祭(2月3日)、初午祭(3月)、秋季大祭(10月30日～11月3日) |本殿 = |札所等 = }}

{{神社 |名称 = 與杼神社 |画像 = [[File:Inabaj1.jpg|本殿|250px]] |所在地 = [[京都市]][[伏見区]]淀本町167 |ISO = JP-26 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 19.2 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 04.3 |ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]]、[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|速秋津姫命]]の三柱） |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = [[応和]]年間（[[961年]]-[[964年]]） |例祭 = 元旦祭(1月1日)、節分祭(2月3日)、初午祭(3月)、秋季大祭(10月30日～11月3日) |本殿 = |札所等 = }}
'''與杼神社'''（よどじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[伏見区]][[淀]]にある神社。祭神は、[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]]、[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|速秋津姫命]]の三柱。
==歴史== [[愛宕念仏寺]]などを再興した[[千観]][[内供]]が[[応和]]年間(961年-964年)頃に[[肥前国]]（現在の佐賀県）[[佐賀郡]][[河上村]]の[[與止日女神社]]より[[與止日女神社#祭神|淀大明神]]を勧請して建立。
==建造物== ===文化財建造物=== *拝殿 1649年頃建立の江戸時代建築で国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。
===かつて存在した建築物=== *宝塔 元禄年間（1688年-1704年）頃に建立（または再建）された[[多宝塔]]。明治初年の[[神仏分離]]で破却されたという。 *本殿 1649年頃の建立で1971年（昭和46年）に重要文化財に指定されたが1975年（昭和50年）8月5日に花火の火による火災で焼失した。1980年（昭和55年）、現在の本殿が再建された。
==参考資料== * [[黒川道祐]]「[[雍州府志]]」（1686年） *「都名所圖會」（1780年）

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 與杼神社 |画像 = [[File:Inabaj1.jpg|本殿|250px]] |所在地 = [[京都府]][[京都市]][[伏見区]]淀本町167 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 54 | 緯度秒 = 19.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 04.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Kyoto city | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]]
[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]
[[ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|速秋津姫命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[応和]]年間（[[961年]]-[[964年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 元旦祭(1月1日)
節分祭(2月3日)
初午祭(3月)
秋季大祭(10月30日～11月3日) |神事 = }} '''與杼神社'''（よどじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[伏見区]][[淀]]にある神社。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 ==祭神== *[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]](トヨタマヒメノミコト) *[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]](タカミムスビノカミ) *[[ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|速秋津姫命]](ハヤアキツヒメノミコト) ==歴史== 社伝等によると[[応和]]年間(961年-964年)頃に[[愛宕念仏寺]]などを再興した[[千観]][[内供]]が[[肥前国]]（現在の佐賀県）[[佐賀郡]][[河上村]]の[[與止日女神社]]より[[與止日女神社#祭神|淀大明神]]を勧請して建立したとされる。しかしながら、[[日本三代実録]]には[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）に従五位下の神位を賜ったという記述があり、延歴5年(927年)に成立した[[延喜式神名帳]]では山城国乙訓郡の小社として列していることから応和年間以前には存在したと考えられる[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 - 京都に乾杯]2019年6月27日　閲覧。 ==境内== ===文化財建造物=== *拝殿　1649年頃建立の江戸時代建築で国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。　 ===かつて存在した建築物=== *宝塔　元禄年間（1688年-1704年）頃に建立（または再建）された[[多宝塔]]。明治初年の[[神仏分離]]で破却されたという。　 *本殿　1649年頃の建立で1971年（昭和46年）に重要文化財に指定された。しかし、1975年（昭和50年）8月5日に中学生による花火の火が檜皮葺屋根に着火しことが原因により火災で焼失した[https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/shobo/page/0000159537.html 昭和50年8月5日　伏見区与杼神社本殿火災 - 京都市]2019年6月27日　閲覧。1980年（昭和55年）、現在の本殿が再建された。 File:Inabaj2.jpg|拝殿 File:Inabaj3.jpg|稲葉神社 File:Yodojinja01.jpg|イチョウと注連縄 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[京都府]][[京都市]][[伏見区]]淀本町167 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[京阪本線]][[淀駅]]下車後、徒歩約3分（南西に約200m） ==参考資料== * [[黒川道祐]]「[[雍州府志]]」（1686年） *「都名所圖會」（1780年） == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Yodo-jinja}} {{osm box|w|388390166}} *[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/yuisyo.html 與杼神社（公式サイト）] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よとしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:伏見区の歴史]] [[Category:伏見区の建築物]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]]

秋季大祭(10月30日～11月3日) |神事 = }} '''與杼神社'''（よどじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[京都市]][[伏見区]][[淀]]にある神社。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 ==祭神== *[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]]（トヨタマヒメノミコト） *[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]（タカミムスビノカミ） *[[ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|速秋津姫命]]（ハヤアキツヒメノミコト） ==歴史== 社伝等によると[[応和]]年間(961年-964年)頃に[[愛宕念仏寺]]などを再興した[[千観]][[内供]]が[[肥前国]]（現在の佐賀県）[[佐賀郡]][[河上村]]の[[與止日女神社]]より[[與止日女神社#祭神|淀大明神]]を勧請して建立したとされる。しかしながら、[[日本三代実録]]には[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）に従五位下の神位を賜ったという記述があり、延歴5年(927年)に成立した[[延喜式神名帳]]では山城国乙訓郡の小社として列していることから応和年間以前には存在したと考えられる[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 - 京都に乾杯]2019年6月27日　閲覧。 ==境内== ===文化財建造物=== *拝殿　1649年頃建立の江戸時代建築で国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。　 ===かつて存在した建築物=== *宝塔　元禄年間（1688年-1704年）頃に建立（または再建）された[[多宝塔]]。明治初年の[[神仏分離]]で破却されたという。　 *本殿　1649年頃の建立で1971年（昭和46年）に重要文化財に指定された。しかし、1975年（昭和50年）8月5日に中学生による花火の火が檜皮葺屋根に着火しことが原因により火災で焼失した[https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/shobo/page/0000159537.html 昭和50年8月5日　伏見区与杼神社本殿火災 - 京都市]2019年6月27日　閲覧。1980年（昭和55年）、現在の本殿が再建された。 File:Yodo-jinja, keidai-2.jpg|拝殿・本殿 File:Inabaj2.jpg|本殿 File:Yodo-jinja, haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Yodo-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|境内社 File:Yodojinja01.jpg|イチョウと注連縄 File:Yodo-jinja, torii.jpg|鳥居 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[京都府]][[京都市]][[伏見区]]淀本町167 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[京阪本線]][[淀駅]]下車後、徒歩約3分（南西に約200m） ==参考資料== * [[黒川道祐]]「[[雍州府志]]」（1686年） *「都名所圖會」（1780年） == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Yodo-jinja}} {{osm box|w|388390166}} *[http://www.yodojinja.com/ 與杼神社（公式サイト）] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よとしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:伏見区の歴史]] [[Category:伏見区の建築物]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]]

初午祭（3月）
秋季大祭（10月30日-11月3日） |神事 = }} '''與杼神社'''（よどじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[伏見区]][[淀]]本町にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。[[淀城]]の本丸跡に建てられている。 == 歴史 == 社伝等によると[[応和]]年間（[[961年]] - [[964年]]）に、[[愛宕念仏寺]]などを再興した[[千観]][[内供]]が[[肥前国]][[佐賀郡]]河上村（現・[[佐賀県]][[佐賀市]]大和町大字川上）の[[與止日女神社]]から[[與止日女神社#祭神|淀大明神]]を勧請して[[山城国]][[乙訓郡]]水垂村（現・伏見区淀大下津町）に建立したとされる。しかしながら、[[日本三代実録]]には[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）に従五位下の神位を賜ったという記述があり、[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）に成立した[[延喜式神名帳]]では山城国乙訓郡の小社として列していることから応和年間以前には存在したと考えられる[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 - 京都に乾杯]2019年6月27日　閲覧。[[桂川 (淀川水系)|桂川]]の水上運輸の守護神とされ、淀姫社や水垂（みずたれ）社とも呼ばれていた。 [[慶長]]12年（[[1607年]]）に[[豊臣秀頼]]によって[[片桐且元]]を奉行として本殿と拝殿が再建された。 しかし、本殿は[[慶安]]2年（[[1649年]]）頃には建て替えられたという。その後[[1902年]]（[[明治]]35年）6月に桂川の河川敷拡幅工事に伴って、当社は現在地である[[淀城]]本丸跡に移転した。それまでの社地は御旅所となっいる。 [[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）8月に[[重要文化財]]であった本殿が中学生の花火遊びにより全焼したが、[[1980年]]（昭和55年）3月に再建された。 当社の南には[[淀藩]]主であった[[稲葉氏]]が、藩祖の[[稲葉正成]]を祀るために建立した稲葉神社が建てられている。 == 祭神 == * [[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]]（トヨタマヒメノミコト） - 別名を與止日女（よどひめのみこと）という。また、[[神功皇后]]の妹であるという。 * [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]（タカミムスビノカミ） * [[ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|速秋津姫命]]（ハヤアキツヒメノミコト） == 境内 == * 本殿 - [[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）3月再建。 * 拝殿（[[重要文化財]]） - [[慶長]]12年（[[1607年]]）、[[豊臣秀頼]]によって[[片桐且元]]を奉行として再建。 * 日大臣社 - [[伊勢神宮]]より[[由貴御倉]]を拝領し、[[1975年]]（昭和50年）から[[1979年]]（昭和54年）まで仮本殿とされた。本殿が再建される際に現在地に移されると日大臣社とされた。 * 高灯籠 - [[大坂]]の豪商[[淀屋]]ゆかりの者による寄進。 * 長姫[[弁才天]]社 * 豊丸社 * 川上社 * 神輿社 - [[2007年]]（[[平成]]19年）再建。 * 社務所 - [[2000年]]（平成12年）9月再建。 * 境外地 ** 御旅所 - 京都府京都市伏見区淀大下津町19-18 File:Yodo-jinja, keidai-2.jpg|本殿・拝殿 File:Inabaj2.jpg|本殿 File:Yodo-jinja, haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Yodo-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|境内社 File:Yodojinja01.jpg|イチョウと注連縄 File:Yodo-jinja, torii.jpg|鳥居 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === * 拝殿　 == かつて存在した建築物 == * [[多宝塔]] - [[元禄]]年間（[[1688年]] - [[1704年]]）に建立（または再建）された多宝塔。[[明治]]初年の[[神仏分離]]で破却されたという。　 * 本殿 - 1971年（昭和46年）に重要文化財に指定された。しかし、1975年（昭和50年）8月5日に中学生による花火の火が檜皮葺の屋根に着火しことが原因により火災で焼失した[https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/shobo/page/0000159537.html 昭和50年8月5日　伏見区与杼神社本殿火災 - 京都市]2019年6月27日　閲覧。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[京都府]][[京都市]][[伏見区]]淀本町167 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[京阪本線]][[淀駅]]下車後、徒歩約3分（南西に約200m） == 参考資料 == * [[黒川道祐]]「[[雍州府志]]」 - [[貞享]]3年（[[1686年]]） *「都名所圖會」 - [[安永 (元号)|安永]]9年（[[1780年]]） == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Yodo-jinja}} {{osm box|w|388390166}} *[http://www.yodojinja.com/ 與杼神社（公式サイト）] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よとしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:伏見区の歴史]] [[Category:伏見区の建築物]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]]

別名を與止日女（よどひめのみこと）という。また、[[神功皇后]]の妹であるという。 * [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]（タカミムスビノカミ） * [[ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|速秋津姫命]]（ハヤアキツヒメノミコト） == 境内 == * 本殿 - [[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）3月再建。 * 拝殿（[[重要文化財]]） - [[慶長]]12年（[[1607年]]）、[[豊臣秀頼]]によって[[片桐且元]]を奉行として再建。 * 日大臣社 - [[伊勢神宮]]より[[由貴御倉]]を拝領し、[[1975年]]（昭和50年）から[[1979年]]（昭和54年）まで仮本殿とされた。本殿が再建される際に現在地に移されると日大臣社とされた。 * 高灯籠 - [[大坂]]の豪商[[淀屋]]ゆかりの者による寄進。 * 長姫[[弁才天]]社 * 豊丸社 * 川上社 * 神輿社 - [[2007年]]（[[平成]]19年）再建。 * 社務所 - [[2000年]]（平成12年）9月再建。 * 境外地 ** 御旅所 - 京都府京都市伏見区淀大下津町19-18 File:Yodo-jinja, keidai-2.jpg|本殿と拝殿 File:Inabaj2.jpg|本殿 File:Yodo-jinja, haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Yodo-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|境内社 File:Yodojinja01.jpg|イチョウと注連縄 File:Yodo-jinja, torii.jpg|鳥居 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === * 拝殿　 == かつて存在した建築物 == * [[多宝塔]] - [[元禄]]年間（[[1688年]] - [[1704年]]）に建立（または再建）された多宝塔。[[明治]]初年の[[神仏分離]]で破却されたという。　 * 本殿 - 1971年（昭和46年）に重要文化財に指定された。しかし、1975年（昭和50年）8月5日に中学生による花火の火が檜皮葺の屋根に着火しことが原因により火災で焼失した[https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/shobo/page/0000159537.html 昭和50年8月5日　伏見区与杼神社本殿火災 - 京都市]2019年6月27日　閲覧。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[京都府]][[京都市]][[伏見区]]淀本町167 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[京阪本線]][[淀駅]]下車後、徒歩約3分（南西に約200m） == 参考資料 == * [[黒川道祐]]「[[雍州府志]]」 - [[貞享]]3年（[[1686年]]） *「都名所圖會」 - [[安永 (元号)|安永]]9年（[[1780年]]） == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Yodo-jinja}} {{osm box|w|388390166}} *[http://www.yodojinja.com/ 與杼神社（公式サイト）] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よとしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:伏見区の歴史]] [[Category:伏見区の建築物]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 與杼神社 |画像 = [[File:Inabaj1.jpg|本殿|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[京都府]][[京都市]][[伏見区]][[淀]]本町167 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 54 | 緯度秒 = 19.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 04.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]]

節分祭（2月3日）
初午祭（3月）
秋季大祭（10月30日-11月3日） |神事 = }} '''與杼神社'''（よどじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[伏見区]][[淀]]本町にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。[[淀城]]の本丸跡に建てられている。 == 歴史 == 社伝等によると[[応和]]年間（[[961年]] - [[964年]]）に、[[愛宕念仏寺]]などを再興した[[千観]][[内供]]が[[肥前国]][[佐賀郡]]河上村（現・[[佐賀県]][[佐賀市]]大和町大字川上）の[[與止日女神社]]から[[與止日女神社#祭神|淀大明神]]を勧請して[[山城国]][[乙訓郡]]水垂村（現・伏見区淀大下津町）に建立したとされる。しかしながら、[[日本三代実録]]には[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）に従五位下の神位を賜ったという記述があり、[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）に成立した[[延喜式神名帳]]では山城国乙訓郡の小社として列していることから応和年間以前には存在したと考えられる[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 - 京都に乾杯]2019年6月27日　閲覧。[[桂川 (淀川水系)|桂川]]の水上運輸の守護神とされ、淀姫社や水垂（みずたれ）社とも呼ばれていた。 [[慶長]]12年（[[1607年]]）に[[豊臣秀頼]]によって[[片桐且元]]を奉行として本殿と拝殿が再建された。しかし、本殿は[[慶安]]2年（[[1649年]]）頃には建て替えられたという。 [[明治]]時代になると[[郷社]]に列せられている。 [[1902年]]（明治35年）6月に桂川の河川敷拡幅工事に伴って、当社は現在地である[[淀城]]本丸跡に移転した。それまでの社地は御旅所となっている。 [[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）8月に[[重要文化財]]であった本殿が中学生の花火遊びにより全焼したが、[[1980年]]（昭和55年）3月に再建された。 当社の南には[[淀藩]]主であった[[稲葉氏]]が、藩祖の[[稲葉正成]]を祀るために建立した稲葉神社が建てられている。 == 祭神 == * [[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]]（トヨタマヒメノミコト） - 別名を與止日女（よどひめのみこと）という。また、[[神功皇后]]の妹であるという。 * [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]（タカミムスビノカミ） * [[ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|速秋津姫命]]（ハヤアキツヒメノミコト） == 境内 == * 本殿 - [[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）3月再建。 * 拝殿（[[重要文化財]]） - [[慶長]]12年（[[1607年]]）、[[豊臣秀頼]]によって[[片桐且元]]を奉行として再建。 * 日大臣社 - [[伊勢神宮]]より[[由貴御倉]]を拝領し、[[1975年]]（昭和50年）から[[1979年]]（昭和54年）まで仮本殿とされた。本殿が再建される際に現在地に移されると日大臣社とされた。 * 高灯籠 - [[大坂]]の豪商[[淀屋]]ゆかりの者による寄進。 * 長姫[[弁才天]]社 * 豊丸社 * 川上社 * 神輿社 - [[2007年]]（[[平成]]19年）再建。 * 社務所 - [[2000年]]（平成12年）9月再建。 * 境外地 ** 御旅所 - revision.text: #redirect[[en:Yodo Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:11]]
#redirect[[en:Yodo Shrine]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects Y]]
{{infobox shinto shrine|image=Inabaj1.jpg}}

{{infobox shinto shrine|image=Inabaj1.jpg}} '''Yodo Shrine''' (與杼神社, '''Yodo Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and under the modern shrine‑ranking system was designated a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社}}. The present grounds occupy the former main bailey of {{ill|Yodo Castle|ja|淀城}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the Ōwa era (961 – 964) the monk‑restorer {{ill|Chikan (Heian monk)|ja|千観}} brought {{ill|Yodohime Myōjin|ja|淀大明神}} from {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|與止日女神社}} in Kawakami, Saga District, [[Hizen Province]] (today Ōaza Kawakami, Yamato‑chō, [[Saga]], [[Saga Prefecture]]), and founded a new shrine at Mizutare in [[Otokuni District]], [[Yamashiro Province]] (now Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku). However, the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' records that the shrine received Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in 859 (Jōgan 1), and the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' completed in 927 lists it as a small shrine of Otokuni District, indicating that it already existed before the Ōwa era.<ref name="乾杯1">[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 ‐ 京都に乾杯] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref> The shrine was revered as the guardian of river transport on the [[Katsura River]] and was also called {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|淀姫社}} or Mizutare Shrine.
In 1607 (Keichō 12) [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] ordered {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元}} to rebuild the honden and haiden. The honden was again reconstructed around 1649 (Keian 2).
During the [[Meiji period]] the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha. In June 1902 (Meiji 35) river‑widening works along the Katsura forced relocation to the present site on Yodo Castle’s honmaru; the former precinct became the shrine’s ''otabisho''.
On 5 August 1975 (Shōwa 50) the Important‑Cultural‑Property honden was destroyed by a fire started by middle‑school students’ fireworks, but it was rebuilt in March 1980 (Shōwa 55).
Immediately south stands {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|稲葉神社}}, founded by the {{ill|Inaba clan|ja|稲葉氏}}, daimyō of the {{ill|Yodo Domain|ja|淀藩}}, to enshrine their ancestor {{ill|Inaba Masanari|ja|稲葉正成}}.
== Enshrined kami == * [[Toyotama‑hime]] (also called '''Yodohime no Mikoto'''); said to be the younger sister of [[Empress Jingū]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命}}
== Precinct == * '''Honden''' – rebuilt March 1980 * '''Haiden''' (Important Cultural Property) – rebuilt 1607 under Toyotomi Hideyori & Katagiri Katsumoto * '''Nichidaijin‑sha''' (日大臣社) – sacred granary from [[Ise Grand Shrine]]; served as temporary honden 1975‑1979, then resited here * '''Tall stone lantern''' – donated by a patron linked to the [[Yodoya]] merchant house of [[Osaka]] * '''Nagahime Benzaiten‑sha''' * '''Toyomaru‑sha''' * '''Kawakami‑sha''' * '''Mikoshi‑sha''' – rebuilt 2007 (Heisei 19) * '''Shrine office''' – rebuilt September 2000 (Heisei 12)
;Off‑site * '''Otabisho''' – 19‑18 Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku, Kyoto

{{infobox shinto shrine|image=Inabaj1.jpg}} '''Yodo Shrine''' (與杼神社, '''Yodo Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and under the modern shrine‑ranking system was designated a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The present grounds occupy the former main bailey of {{ill|Yodo Castle|ja|淀城|1=Yodo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559695}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the Ōwa era (961 – 964) the monk‑restorer {{ill|Chikan (Heian monk)|ja|千観|1=Senkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406805}} brought {{ill|Yodohime Myōjin|ja|淀大明神}} from {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|與止日女神社}} in Kawakami, Saga District, [[Hizen Province]] (today Ōaza Kawakami, Yamato‑chō, [[Saga]], [[Saga Prefecture]]), and founded a new shrine at Mizutare in [[Otokuni District]], [[Yamashiro Province]] (now Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku). However, the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' records that the shrine received Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in 859 (Jōgan 1), and the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' completed in 927 lists it as a small shrine of Otokuni District, indicating that it already existed before the Ōwa era.<ref name="乾杯1">[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 ‐ 京都に乾杯] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref> The shrine was revered as the guardian of river transport on the [[Katsura River]] and was also called {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|淀姫社}} or Mizutare Shrine.
In 1607 (Keichō 12) [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] ordered {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|1=Katagiri Katsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1882775}} to rebuild the honden and haiden. The honden was again reconstructed around 1649 (Keian 2).
During the [[Meiji period]] the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha. In June 1902 (Meiji 35) river‑widening works along the Katsura forced relocation to the present site on Yodo Castle’s honmaru; the former precinct became the shrine’s ''otabisho''.
On 5 August 1975 (Shōwa 50) the Important‑Cultural‑Property honden was destroyed by a fire started by middle‑school students’ fireworks, but it was rebuilt in March 1980 (Shōwa 55).
Immediately south stands {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|稲葉神社}}, founded by the {{ill|Inaba clan|ja|稲葉氏|1=Inaba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975276}}, daimyō of the {{ill|Yodo Domain|ja|淀藩|1=Yodo Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844014}}, to enshrine their ancestor {{ill|Inaba Masanari|ja|稲葉正成|1=Inaba Masanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4352518}}.
== Enshrined kami == * [[Toyotama‑hime]] (also called '''Yodohime no Mikoto'''); said to be the younger sister of [[Empress Jingū]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|1=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11326733}}
== Precinct == * '''Honden''' – rebuilt March 1980 * '''Haiden''' (Important Cultural Property) – rebuilt 1607 under Toyotomi Hideyori & Katagiri Katsumoto * '''Nichidaijin‑sha''' (日大臣社) – sacred granary from [[Ise Grand Shrine]]; served as temporary honden 1975‑1979, then resited here * '''Tall stone lantern''' – donated by a patron linked to the [[Yodoya]] merchant house of [[Osaka]] * '''Nagahime Benzaiten‑sha''' * '''Toyomaru‑sha''' * '''Kawakami‑sha''' * '''Mikoshi‑sha''' – rebuilt 2007 (Heisei 19) * '''Shrine office''' – rebuilt September 2000 (Heisei 12)
;Off‑site * '''Otabisho''' – 19‑18 Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku, Kyoto

== Location == * 167 Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan
== Access == * 3‑minute walk (≈ 200 m SW) from [[Yodo Station]] on the [[Keihan Main Line]]
== References == * {{ill|Kurokawa Dōsuke|ja|黒川道祐|1=Kurokawa Dōyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6562509}}, ''[[Yōshū Fushi]]'' (1686) * ''Miyako Meisho Zue'' (1780) {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{複数の問題 | 出典の明記 = 2023年11月25日 (土) 09:10 (UTC) | 独自研究 = 2023年11月25日 (土) 09:10 (UTC) }} {{神社 |名称 = 與杼神社 |画像 = [[File:Inabaj1.jpg|本殿|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[京都府]][[京都市]][[伏見区]][[淀]]本町167 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 54 | 緯度秒 = 19.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 04.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]]
[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]
[[ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|速秋津姫命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[応和]]年間（[[961年]] - [[964年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 元旦祭（1月1日）
節分祭（2月3日）
初午祭（3月）
秋季大祭（10月30日-11月3日） |神事 = }} '''與杼神社'''（よどじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[伏見区]][[淀]]本町にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。[[淀城]]の本丸跡に建てられている。 == 歴史 == 社伝等によると[[応和]]年間（[[961年]] - [[964年]]）に、[[愛宕念仏寺]]などを再興した[[千観]][[内供]]が[[肥前国]][[佐賀郡]]河上村（現・[[佐賀県]][[佐賀市]]大和町大字川上）の[[與止日女神社]]から[[與止日女神社#祭神|淀大明神]]を勧請して[[山城国]][[乙訓郡]]水垂村（現・伏見区淀大下津町）に建立したとされる。しかしながら、[[日本三代実録]]には[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）に従五位下の神位を賜ったという記述があり、[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）に成立した[[延喜式神名帳]]では山城国乙訓郡の小社として列していることから応和年間以前には存在したと考えられる[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 - 京都に乾杯]2019年6月27日　閲覧。[[桂川 (淀川水系)|桂川]]の水上運輸の守護神とされ、淀姫社や水垂（みずたれ）社とも呼ばれていた。 [[慶長]]12年（[[1607年]]）に[[豊臣秀頼]]によって[[片桐且元]]を奉行として本殿と拝殿が再建された。しかし、本殿は[[慶安]]2年（[[1649年]]）頃には建て替えられたという。 [[明治]]時代になると[[郷社]]に列せられている。 [[1902年]]（明治35年）6月に桂川の河川敷拡幅工事に伴って、当社は現在地である[[淀城]]本丸跡に移転した。それまでの社地は御旅所となっている。 [[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）8月に[[重要文化財]]であった本殿が中学生の花火遊びにより全焼したが、[[1980年]]（昭和55年）3月に再建された。 当社の南には[[淀藩]]主であった[[稲葉氏]]が、藩祖の[[稲葉正成]]を祀るために建立した稲葉神社が建てられている。 == 祭神 == * [[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]]（トヨタマヒメノミコト） - 別名を與止日女（よどひめのみこと）という。また、[[神功皇后]]の妹であるという。 * [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]（タカミムスビノカミ） * [[ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|速秋津姫命]]（ハヤアキツヒメノミコト） == 境内 == * 本殿 - [[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）3月再建。 * 拝殿（[[重要文化財]]） - [[慶長]]12年（[[1607年]]）、[[豊臣秀頼]]によって[[片桐且元]]を奉行として再建。 * 日大臣社 - [[伊勢神宮]]より[[由貴御倉]]を拝領し、[[1975年]]（昭和50年）から[[1979年]]（昭和54年）まで仮本殿とされた。本殿が再建される際に現在地に移されると日大臣社とされた。 * 高灯籠 - [[大坂]]の豪商[[淀屋]]ゆかりの者による寄進。 * 長姫[[弁才天]]社 * 豊丸社 * 川上社 * 神輿社 - [[2007年]]（[[平成]]19年）再建。 * 社務所 - [[2000年]]（平成12年）9月再建。 * 境外地 ** 御旅所 - 京都府京都市伏見区淀大下津町19-18 File:Yodo-jinja, keidai-2.jpg|本殿と拝殿 File:Inabaj2.jpg|本殿 File:Yodo-jinja, haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Yodo-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|境内社 File:Yodojinja01.jpg|イチョウと注連縄 File:Yodo-jinja, torii.jpg|鳥居 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === * 拝殿　 == かつて存在した建築物 == * [[多宝塔]] - [[元禄]]年間（[[1688年]] - [[1704年]]）に建立（または再建）された多宝塔。[[明治]]初年の[[神仏分離]]で破却されたという。　 * 本殿 - 1971年（昭和46年）に重要文化財に指定された。しかし、1975年（昭和50年）8月5日に中学生による花火の火が檜皮葺の屋根に着火したことが原因により火災で焼失した[https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/shobo/page/0000159537.html 昭和50年8月5日　伏見区与杼神社本殿火災 - 京都市]2019年6月27日　閲覧。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[京都府]][[京都市]][[伏見区]]淀本町167 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[京阪本線]][[淀駅]]下車後、徒歩約3分（南西に約200m） == 参考資料 == * [[黒川道祐]]「[[雍州府志]]」 - [[貞享]]3年（[[1686年]]） *「都名所圖會」 - [[安永 (元号)|安永]]9年（[[1780年]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Yodo-jinja}} {{osm box|w|388390166}} * {{official website}} * {{京都観光Navi|category_id=9|tourism_id=437|name=与杼神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よとしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:伏見区の歴史]] [[Category:伏見区の建築物]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]]

節分祭（2月3日）
初午祭（3月）
秋季大祭（10月30日-11月3日） |神事 = }} '''與杼神社'''（よどじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[伏見区]][[淀]]本町にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。[[淀城]]の本丸跡に建てられている。 == 歴史 == 社伝等によると[[応和]]年間（[[961年]] - [[964年]]）に、[[愛宕念仏寺]]などを再興した[[千観]][[内供]]が[[肥前国]][[佐賀郡]]河上村（現・[[佐賀県]][[佐賀市]]大和町大字川上）の[[與止日女神社]]から[[與止日女神社#祭神|淀大明神]]を勧請して[[山城国]][[乙訓郡]]水垂村（現・伏見区淀大下津町）に建立したとされる。しかしながら、[[日本三代実録]]には[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）に従五位下の神位を賜ったという記述があり、[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）に成立した[[延喜式神名帳]]では山城国乙訓郡の小社として列していることから応和年間以前には存在したと考えられる[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 - 京都に乾杯]2019年6月27日　閲覧。[[桂川 (淀川水系)|桂川]]の水上運輸の守護神とされ、淀姫社や水垂（みずたれ）社とも呼ばれていた。 [[慶長]]12年（[[1607年]]）に[[豊臣秀頼]]によって[[片桐且元]]を奉行として本殿と拝殿が再建された。しかし、本殿は[[慶安]]2年（[[1649年]]）頃には建て替えられたという。 [[明治]]時代になると[[郷社]]に列せられている。 [[1902年]]（明治35年）6月に桂川の河川敷拡幅工事に伴って、当社は現在地である[[淀城]]本丸跡に移転した。それまでの社地は御旅所となっている。 [[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）8月に[[重要文化財]]であった本殿が中学生の花火遊びにより全焼したが、[[1980年]]（昭和55年）3月に再建された。 当社の南には[[淀藩]]主であった[[稲葉氏]]が、藩祖の[[稲葉正成]]を祀るために建立した稲葉神社が建てられている。 == 祭神 == * [[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]]（トヨタマヒメノミコト） - 別名を與止日女（よどひめのみこと）という。また、[[神功皇后]]の妹であるという。 * [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]（タカミムスビノカミ） * [[ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|速秋津姫命]]（ハヤアキツヒメノミコト） == 境内 == * 本殿 - [[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）3月再建。 * 拝殿（[[重要文化財]]） - [[慶長]]12年（[[1607年]]）、[[豊臣秀頼]]によって[[片桐且元]]を奉行として再建。 * 日大臣社 - [[伊勢神宮]]より[[由貴御倉]]を拝領し、[[1975年]]（昭和50年）から[[1979年]]（昭和54年）まで仮本殿とされた。本殿が再建される際に現在地に移されると日大臣社とされた。 * 高灯籠 - [[大坂]]の豪商[[淀屋]]ゆかりの者による寄進。 * 長姫[[弁才天]]社 * 豊丸社 * 川上社 * 神輿社 - [[2007年]]（[[平成]]19年）再建。 * 社務所 - [[2000年]]（平成12年）9月再建。 * 境外地 ** 御旅所 - 京都府京都市伏見区淀大下津町19-18 File:Yodo-jinja, keidai-2.jpg|本殿と拝殿 File:Inabaj2.jpg|本殿 File:Yodo-jinja, haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Yodo-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|境内社 File:Yodojinja01.jpg|イチョウと注連縄 File:Yodo-jinja, torii.jpg|鳥居 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === * 拝殿　 == かつて存在した建築物 == * [[多宝塔]] - [[元禄]]年間（[[1688年]] - [[1704年]]）に建立（または再建）された多宝塔。[[明治]]初年の[[神仏分離]]で破却されたという。　 * 本殿 - 1971年（昭和46年）に重要文化財に指定された。しかし、1975年（昭和50年）8月5日に中学生による花火の火が檜皮葺の屋根に着火したことが原因により火災で焼失した[https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/shobo/page/0000159537.html 昭和50年8月5日　伏見区与杼神社本殿火災 - 京都市]2019年6月27日　閲覧。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[京都府]][[京都市]][[伏見区]]淀本町167 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[京阪本線]][[淀駅]]下車後、徒歩約3分（南西に約200m） == 参考資料 == * [[黒川道祐]]「[[雍州府志]]」 - [[貞享]]3年（[[1686年]]） *「都名所圖會」 - [[安永 (元号)|安永]]9年（[[1780年]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Yodo-jinja}} {{osm box|w|388390166}} * {{official website}} * {{京都観光Navi|category_id=9|tourism_id=437|name=与杼神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よとしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:伏見区の歴史]] [[Category:伏見区の建築物]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]]

{{infobox shinto shrine|image=Inabaj1.jpg}} '''Yodo Shrine''' (與杼神社, '''Yodo Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and under the modern shrine‑ranking system was designated a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The present grounds occupy the former main bailey of {{ill|Yodo Castle|ja|淀城|1=Yodo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559695}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the Ōwa era (961 – 964) the monk‑restorer {{ill|Chikan (Heian monk)|ja|千観|1=Senkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406805}} brought {{ill|Yodohime Myōjin|ja|淀大明神}} from {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|與止日女神社}} in Kawakami, Saga District, [[Hizen Province]] (today Ōaza Kawakami, Yamato‑chō, [[Saga]], [[Saga Prefecture]]), and founded a new shrine at Mizutare in [[Otokuni District]], [[Yamashiro Province]] (now Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku). However, the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' records that the shrine received Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in 859 (Jōgan 1), and the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' completed in 927 lists it as a small shrine of Otokuni District, indicating that it already existed before the Ōwa era.<ref name="乾杯1">[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 ‐ 京都に乾杯] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref> The shrine was revered as the guardian of river transport on the [[Katsura River]] and was also called {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|淀姫社}} or Mizutare Shrine.
In 1607 (Keichō 12) [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] ordered {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|1=Katagiri Katsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1882775}} to rebuild the honden and haiden. The honden was again reconstructed around 1649 (Keian 2).
During the [[Meiji period]] the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha. In June 1902 (Meiji 35) river‑widening works along the Katsura forced relocation to the present site on Yodo Castle’s honmaru; the former precinct became the shrine’s ''otabisho''.
On 5 August 1975 (Shōwa 50) the Important‑Cultural‑Property honden was destroyed by a fire started by middle‑school students’ fireworks, but it was rebuilt in March 1980 (Shōwa 55).
Immediately south stands {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|稲葉神社}}, founded by the {{ill|Inaba clan|ja|稲葉氏|1=Inaba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975276}}, daimyō of the {{ill|Yodo Domain|ja|淀藩|1=Yodo Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844014}}, to enshrine their ancestor {{ill|Inaba Masanari|ja|稲葉正成|1=Inaba Masanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4352518}}.
== Enshrined kami == * [[Toyotama‑hime]] (also called '''Yodohime no Mikoto'''); said to be the younger sister of [[Empress Jingū]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|1=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11326733}}
== Precinct == * '''Honden''' – rebuilt March 1980 * '''Haiden''' (Important Cultural Property) – rebuilt 1607 under Toyotomi Hideyori & Katagiri Katsumoto * '''Nichidaijin‑sha''' (日大臣社) – sacred granary from [[Ise Grand Shrine]]; served as temporary honden 1975‑1979, then resited here * '''Tall stone lantern''' – donated by a patron linked to the [[Yodoya]] merchant house of [[Osaka]] * '''Nagahime Benzaiten‑sha''' * '''Toyomaru‑sha''' * '''Kawakami‑sha''' * '''Mikoshi‑sha''' – rebuilt 2007 (Heisei 19) * '''Shrine office''' – rebuilt September 2000 (Heisei 12)
;Off‑site * '''Otabisho''' – 19‑18 Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku, Kyoto
<gallery> File:Yodo-jinja, keidai-2.jpg|Honden & Haiden File:Inabaj2.jpg|Honden File:Yodo-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Yodo-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Auxiliary shrines File:Yodojinja01.jpg|Ginkgo tree with shimenawa File:Yodo-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii </gallery>
== Cultural properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Haiden
== Former structures == * '''Tahōtō''' – a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔}} erected (or rebuilt) during the Genroku era (1688–1704); dismantled at the start of the Meiji period during [[shinbutsu bunri]] * '''Honden''' – designated an Important Cultural Property in 1971 but lost to fire on 5 Aug 1975 when fireworks ignited its cypress‑bark roof<ref name="京都市1">[https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/shobo/page/0000159537.html 昭和50年8月5日 伏見区与杼神社本殿火災 ‐ 京都市] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref>
== Location == * 167 Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan
== Access == * 3‑minute walk (≈ 200 m SW) from [[Yodo Station]] on the [[Keihan Main Line]]
== References == * {{ill|Kurokawa Dōsuke|ja|黒川道祐|1=Kurokawa Dōyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6562509}}, ''[[Yōshū Fushi]]'' (1686) * ''Miyako Meisho Zue'' (1780) {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External links == {{Commonscat|Yodo-jinja}} {{osm box|w|388390166}} * {{official website}} * {{京都観光Navi|category_id=9|tourism_id=437|name=与杼神社}}

== Access == * 3‑minute walk (≈ 200 m SW) from [[Yodo Station]] on the [[Keihan Main Line]]
== References == * {{ill|Kurokawa Dōsuke|ja|黒川道祐|1=Kurokawa Dōyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6562509}}, ''[[Yōshū Fushi]]'' (1686) * ''Miyako Meisho Zue'' (1780) {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External links == {{Commonscat|Yodo-jinja}} {{osm box|w|388390166}} * {{official website}} * {{京都観光Navi|category_id=9|tourism_id=437|name=与杼神社}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Shikinaisha]] to [[Category:Shikinaisha]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] removing this because they are all categorized in the subcategories using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{infobox shinto shrine|image=Inabaj1.jpg}} '''Yodo Shrine''' (與杼神社, '''Yodo Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and under the modern shrine‑ranking system was designated a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The present grounds occupy the former main bailey of {{ill|Yodo Castle|ja|淀城|1=Yodo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559695}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the Ōwa era (961 – 964) the monk‑restorer {{ill|Chikan (Heian monk)|ja|千観|1=Senkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406805}} brought {{ill|Yodohime Myōjin|ja|淀大明神}} from {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|與止日女神社}} in Kawakami, Saga District, [[Hizen Province]] (today Ōaza Kawakami, Yamato‑chō, [[Saga]], [[Saga Prefecture]]), and founded a new shrine at Mizutare in [[Otokuni District]], [[Yamashiro Province]] (now Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku). However, the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' records that the shrine received Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in 859 (Jōgan 1), and the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' completed in 927 lists it as a small shrine of Otokuni District, indicating that it already existed before the Ōwa era.<ref name="乾杯1">[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 ‐ 京都に乾杯] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref> The shrine was revered as the guardian of river transport on the [[Katsura River]] and was also called {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|淀姫社}} or Mizutare Shrine.
In 1607 (Keichō 12) [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] ordered {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|1=Katagiri Katsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1882775}} to rebuild the honden and haiden. The honden was again reconstructed around 1649 (Keian 2).
During the [[Meiji period]] the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha. In June 1902 (Meiji 35) river‑widening works along the Katsura forced relocation to the present site on Yodo Castle’s honmaru; the former precinct became the shrine’s ''otabisho''.
On 5 August 1975 (Shōwa 50) the Important‑Cultural‑Property honden was destroyed by a fire started by middle‑school students’ fireworks, but it was rebuilt in March 1980 (Shōwa 55).
Immediately south stands {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|稲葉神社}}, founded by the {{ill|Inaba clan|ja|稲葉氏|1=Inaba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975276}}, daimyō of the {{ill|Yodo Domain|ja|淀藩|1=Yodo Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844014}}, to enshrine their ancestor {{ill|Inaba Masanari|ja|稲葉正成|1=Inaba Masanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4352518}}.
== Enshrined kami == * [[Toyotama‑hime]] (also called '''Yodohime no Mikoto'''); said to be the younger sister of [[Empress Jingū]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|1=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11326733}}
== Precinct == * '''Honden''' – rebuilt March 1980 * '''Haiden''' (Important Cultural Property) – rebuilt 1607 under Toyotomi Hideyori & Katagiri Katsumoto * '''Nichidaijin‑sha''' (日大臣社) – sacred granary from [[Ise Grand Shrine]]; served as temporary honden 1975‑1979, then resited here * '''Tall stone lantern''' – donated by a patron linked to the [[Yodoya]] merchant house of [[Osaka]] * '''Nagahime Benzaiten‑sha''' * '''Toyomaru‑sha''' * '''Kawakami‑sha''' * '''Mikoshi‑sha''' – rebuilt 2007 (Heisei 19) * '''Shrine office''' – rebuilt September 2000 (Heisei 12)
;Off‑site * '''Otabisho''' – 19‑18 Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku, Kyoto

== Location == * 167 Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan
== Access == * 3‑minute walk (≈ 200 m SW) from [[Yodo Station]] on the [[Keihan Main Line]]
== References == * {{ill|Kurokawa Dōsuke|ja|黒川道祐|1=Kurokawa Dōyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6562509}}, ''[[Yōshū Fushi]]'' (1686) * ''Miyako Meisho Zue'' (1780) {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

== Precinct == * '''Honden''' – rebuilt March 1980 * '''Haiden''' (Important Cultural Property) – rebuilt 1607 under Toyotomi Hideyori & Katagiri Katsumoto * '''Nichidaijin‑sha''' (日大臣社) – sacred granary from [[Ise Grand Shrine]]; served as temporary honden 1975‑1979, then resited here * '''Tall stone lantern''' – donated by a patron linked to the [[Yodoya]] merchant house of [[Osaka]] * '''Nagahime Benzaiten‑sha''' * '''Toyomaru‑sha''' * '''Kawakami‑sha''' * '''Mikoshi‑sha''' – rebuilt 2007 (Heisei 19) * '''Shrine office''' – rebuilt September 2000 (Heisei 12)
;Off‑site * '''Otabisho''' – 19‑18 Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku, Kyoto

{{infobox shinto shrine|image=Inabaj1.jpg}} '''Yodo Shrine''' (與杼神社, '''Yodo Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and under the modern shrine‑ranking system was designated a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The present grounds occupy the former main bailey of {{ill|Yodo Castle|ja|淀城|1=Yodo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559695}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the Ōwa era (961 – 964) the monk‑restorer {{ill|Chikan (Heian monk)|ja|千観|1=Senkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406805}} brought {{ill|Yodohime Myōjin|ja|淀大明神}} from {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|與止日女神社}} in Kawakami, Saga District, [[Hizen Province]] (today Ōaza Kawakami, Yamato‑chō, [[Saga]], [[Saga Prefecture]]), and founded a new shrine at Mizutare in [[Otokuni District]], [[Yamashiro Province]] (now Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku). However, the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' records that the shrine received Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in 859 (Jōgan 1), and the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' completed in 927 lists it as a small shrine of Otokuni District, indicating that it already existed before the Ōwa era.<ref name="乾杯1">[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 ‐ 京都に乾杯] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref> The shrine was revered as the guardian of river transport on the [[Katsura River]] and was also called {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|淀姫社}} or Mizutare Shrine.
In 1607 (Keichō 12) [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] ordered {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|1=Katagiri Katsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1882775}} to rebuild the honden and haiden. The honden was again reconstructed around 1649 (Keian 2).
During the [[Meiji period]] the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha. In June 1902 (Meiji 35) river‑widening works along the Katsura forced relocation to the present site on Yodo Castle’s honmaru; the former precinct became the shrine’s ''otabisho''.
On 5 August 1975 (Shōwa 50) the Important‑Cultural‑Property honden was destroyed by a fire started by middle‑school students’ fireworks, but it was rebuilt in March 1980 (Shōwa 55).
Immediately south stands {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|稲葉神社}}, founded by the {{ill|Inaba clan|ja|稲葉氏|1=Inaba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975276}}, daimyō of the {{ill|Yodo Domain|ja|淀藩|1=Yodo Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844014}}, to enshrine their ancestor {{ill|Inaba Masanari|ja|稲葉正成|1=Inaba Masanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4352518}}.
== Enshrined kami == * [[Toyotama‑hime]] (also called '''Yodohime no Mikoto'''); said to be the younger sister of [[Empress Jingū]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|1=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11326733}}
== Precinct == * '''Honden''' – rebuilt March 1980 * '''Haiden''' (Important Cultural Property) – rebuilt 1607 under Toyotomi Hideyori & Katagiri Katsumoto * '''Nichidaijin‑sha''' (日大臣社) – sacred granary from [[Ise Grand Shrine]]; served as temporary honden 1975‑1979, then resited here * '''Tall stone lantern''' – donated by a patron linked to the [[Yodoya]] merchant house of [[Osaka]] * '''Nagahime Benzaiten‑sha''' * '''Toyomaru‑sha''' * '''Kawakami‑sha''' * '''Mikoshi‑sha''' – rebuilt 2007 (Heisei 19) * '''Shrine office''' – rebuilt September 2000 (Heisei 12)
;Off‑site * '''Otabisho''' – 19‑18 Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku, Kyoto

However, the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' records that the shrine received Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in 859 (Jōgan 1), and the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' completed in 927 lists it as a small shrine of Otokuni District, indicating that it already existed before the Ōwa era.<ref name="乾杯1">[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 ‐ 京都に乾杯] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref> The shrine was revered as the guardian of river transport on the [[Katsura River]] and was also called {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|淀姫社}} or Mizutare Shrine.
In 1607 (Keichō 12) [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] ordered {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|1=Katagiri Katsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1882775}} to rebuild the honden and haiden. The honden was again reconstructed around 1649 (Keian 2).
During the [[Meiji period]] the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha. In June 1902 (Meiji 35) river‑widening works along the Katsura forced relocation to the present site on Yodo Castle’s honmaru; the former precinct became the shrine’s ''otabisho''.
On 5 August 1975 (Shōwa 50) the Important‑Cultural‑Property honden was destroyed by a fire started by middle‑school students’ fireworks, but it was rebuilt in March 1980 (Shōwa 55).
Immediately south stands {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|稲葉神社}}, founded by the {{ill|Inaba clan|ja|稲葉氏|1=Inaba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975276}}, daimyō of the {{ill|Yodo Domain|ja|淀藩|1=Yodo Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844014}}, to enshrine their ancestor {{ill|Inaba Masanari|ja|稲葉正成|1=Inaba Masanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4352518}}.
== Enshrined kami == * [[Toyotama‑hime]] (also called '''Yodohime no Mikoto'''); said to be the younger sister of [[Empress Jingū]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|1=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11326733}}
== Precinct == * '''Honden''' – rebuilt March 1980 * '''Haiden''' (Important Cultural Property) – rebuilt 1607 under Toyotomi Hideyori & Katagiri Katsumoto * '''Nichidaijin‑sha''' (日大臣社) – sacred granary from [[Ise Grand Shrine]]; served as temporary honden 1975‑1979, then resited here * '''Tall stone lantern''' – donated by a patron linked to the [[Yodoya]] merchant house of [[Osaka]] * '''Nagahime Benzaiten‑sha''' * '''Toyomaru‑sha''' * '''Kawakami‑sha''' * '''Mikoshi‑sha''' – rebuilt 2007 (Heisei 19) * '''Shrine office''' – rebuilt September 2000 (Heisei 12)
;Off‑site * '''Otabisho''' – 19‑18 Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku, Kyoto

== Cultural properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Haiden
== Former structures == * '''Tahōtō''' – a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔}} erected (or rebuilt) during the Genroku era (1688–1704); dismantled at the start of the Meiji period during [[shinbutsu bunri]] * '''Honden''' – designated an Important Cultural Property in 1971 but lost to fire on 5 Aug 1975 when fireworks ignited its cypress‑bark roof<ref name="京都市1">[https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/shobo/page/0000159537.html 昭和50年8月5日 伏見区与杼神社本殿火災 ‐ 京都市] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref>
== Location == * 167 Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan
== Access == * 3‑minute walk (≈ 200 m SW) from [[Yodo Station]] on the [[Keihan Main Line]]
== References == * {{ill|Kurokawa Dōsuke|ja|黒川道祐|1=Kurokawa Dōyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6562509}}, ''[[Yōshū Fushi]]'' (1686) * ''Miyako Meisho Zue'' (1780) {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yodo-jinja]][[ja:與杼神社]] {{infobox shinto shrine|image=Inabaj1.jpg}} '''Yodo Shrine''' (與杼神社, '''Yodo Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and under the modern shrine‑ranking system was designated a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The present grounds occupy the former main bailey of {{ill|Yodo Castle|ja|淀城|1=Yodo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559695}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the Ōwa era (961 – 964) the monk‑restorer {{ill|Chikan (Heian monk)|ja|千観|1=Senkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406805}} brought {{ill|Yodohime Myōjin|ja|淀大明神}} from {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|與止日女神社}} in Kawakami, Saga District, [[Hizen Province]] (today Ōaza Kawakami, Yamato‑chō, [[Saga]], [[Saga Prefecture]]), and founded a new shrine at Mizutare in [[Otokuni District]], [[Yamashiro Province]] (now Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku). However, the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' records that the shrine received Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in 859 (Jōgan 1), and the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' completed in 927 lists it as a small shrine of Otokuni District, indicating that it already existed before the Ōwa era.<ref name="乾杯1">[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 ‐ 京都に乾杯] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref> The shrine was revered as the guardian of river transport on the [[Katsura River]] and was also called {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|淀姫社}} or Mizutare Shrine.
In 1607 (Keichō 12) [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] ordered {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|1=Katagiri Katsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1882775}} to rebuild the honden and haiden. The honden was again reconstructed around 1649 (Keian 2).
During the [[Meiji period]] the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha. In June 1902 (Meiji 35) river‑widening works along the Katsura forced relocation to the present site on Yodo Castle’s honmaru; the former precinct became the shrine’s ''otabisho''.
On 5 August 1975 (Shōwa 50) the Important‑Cultural‑Property honden was destroyed by a fire started by middle‑school students’ fireworks, but it was rebuilt in March 1980 (Shōwa 55).
Immediately south stands {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|稲葉神社}}, founded by the {{ill|Inaba clan|ja|稲葉氏|1=Inaba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975276}}, daimyō of the {{ill|Yodo Domain|ja|淀藩|1=Yodo Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844014}}, to enshrine their ancestor {{ill|Inaba Masanari|ja|稲葉正成|1=Inaba Masanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4352518}}.
== Enshrined kami == * [[Toyotama‑hime]] (also called '''Yodohime no Mikoto'''); said to be the younger sister of [[Empress Jingū]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|1=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11326733}}
== Precinct == * '''Honden''' – rebuilt March 1980 * '''Haiden''' (Important Cultural Property) – rebuilt 1607 under Toyotomi Hideyori & Katagiri Katsumoto * '''Nichidaijin‑sha''' (日大臣社) – sacred granary from [[Ise Grand Shrine]]; served as temporary honden 1975‑1979, then resited here * '''Tall stone lantern''' – donated by a patron linked to the [[Yodoya]] merchant house of [[Osaka]] * '''Nagahime Benzaiten‑sha''' * '''Toyomaru‑sha''' * '''Kawakami‑sha''' * '''Mikoshi‑sha''' – rebuilt 2007 (Heisei 19) * '''Shrine office''' – rebuilt September 2000 (Heisei 12)
;Off‑site * '''Otabisho''' – 19‑18 Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku, Kyoto
<gallery> File:Yodo-jinja, keidai-2.jpg|Honden & Haiden File:Inabaj2.jpg|Honden File:Yodo-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Yodo-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Auxiliary shrines File:Yodojinja01.jpg|Ginkgo tree with shimenawa File:Yodo-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii </gallery>
== Cultural properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Haiden
== Former structures == * '''Tahōtō''' – a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔}} erected (or rebuilt) during the Genroku era (1688–1704); dismantled at the start of the Meiji period during [[shinbutsu bunri]] * '''Honden''' – designated an Important Cultural Property in 1971 but lost to fire on 5 Aug 1975 when fireworks ignited its cypress‑bark roof<ref name="京都市1">[https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/shobo/page/0000159537.html 昭和50年8月5日 伏見区与杼神社本殿火災 ‐ 京都市] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref>
== Location == * 167 Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan
== Access == * 3‑minute walk (≈ 200 m SW) from [[Yodo Station]] on the [[Keihan Main Line]]
== References == * {{ill|Kurokawa Dōsuke|ja|黒川道祐|1=Kurokawa Dōyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6562509}}, ''[[Yōshū Fushi]]'' (1686) * ''Miyako Meisho Zue'' (1780) {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External links == {{Commonscat|Yodo-jinja}} {{osm box|w|388390166}} * {{official website}} * {{京都観光Navi|category_id=9|tourism_id=437|name=与杼神社}}

== Former structures == * '''Tahōtō''' – a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔}} erected (or rebuilt) during the Genroku era (1688–1704); dismantled at the start of the Meiji period during [[shinbutsu bunri]] * '''Honden''' – designated an Important Cultural Property in 1971 but lost to fire on 5 Aug 1975 when fireworks ignited its cypress‑bark roof<ref name="京都市1">[https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/shobo/page/0000159537.html 昭和50年8月5日 伏見区与杼神社本殿火災 ‐ 京都市] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref>
== Location == * 167 Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan
== Access == * 3‑minute walk (≈ 200 m SW) from [[Yodo Station]] on the [[Keihan Main Line]]
== References == * {{ill|Kurokawa Dōsuke|ja|黒川道祐|1=Kurokawa Dōyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6562509}}, ''[[Yōshū Fushi]]'' (1686) * ''Miyako Meisho Zue'' (1780) {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External links == {{Commonscat|Yodo-jinja}} {{osm box|w|388390166}} * {{official website}} * {{京都観光Navi|category_id=9|tourism_id=437|name=与杼神社}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yodo-jinja]][[ja:與杼神社]] {{infobox shinto shrine|image=Inabaj1.jpg}} '''Yodo Shrine''' (與杼神社, '''Yodo Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and under the modern shrine‑ranking system was designated a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The present grounds occupy the former main bailey of {{ill|Yodo Castle|ja|淀城|1=Yodo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559695}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the Ōwa era (961 – 964) the monk‑restorer {{ill|Chikan (Heian monk)|ja|千観|1=Senkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406805}} brought {{ill|Yodohime Myōjin|ja|淀大明神}} from {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|與止日女神社}} in Kawakami, Saga District, [[Hizen Province]] (today Ōaza Kawakami, Yamato‑chō, [[Saga]], [[Saga Prefecture]]), and founded a new shrine at Mizutare in [[Otokuni District]], [[Yamashiro Province]] (now Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku). However, the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' records that the shrine received Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in 859 (Jōgan 1), and the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' completed in 927 lists it as a small shrine of Otokuni District, indicating that it already existed before the Ōwa era.<ref name="乾杯1">[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 ‐ 京都に乾杯] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref> The shrine was revered as the guardian of river transport on the [[Katsura River]] and was also called {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|淀姫社}} or Mizutare Shrine.
In 1607 (Keichō 12) [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] ordered {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|1=Katagiri Katsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1882775}} to rebuild the honden and haiden. The honden was again reconstructed around 1649 (Keian 2).
During the [[Meiji period]] the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha. In June 1902 (Meiji 35) river‑widening works along the Katsura forced relocation to the present site on Yodo Castle’s honmaru; the former precinct became the shrine’s ''otabisho''.
On 5 August 1975 (Shōwa 50) the Important‑Cultural‑Property honden was destroyed by a fire started by middle‑school students’ fireworks, but it was rebuilt in March 1980 (Shōwa 55).
Immediately south stands {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|稲葉神社}}, founded by the {{ill|Inaba clan|ja|稲葉氏|1=Inaba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975276}}, daimyō of the {{ill|Yodo Domain|ja|淀藩|1=Yodo Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844014}}, to enshrine their ancestor {{ill|Inaba Masanari|ja|稲葉正成|1=Inaba Masanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4352518}}.
== Enshrined kami == * [[Toyotama‑hime]] (also called '''Yodohime no Mikoto'''); said to be the younger sister of [[Empress Jingū]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|1=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11326733}}
== Precinct == * '''Honden''' – rebuilt March 1980 * '''Haiden''' (Important Cultural Property) – rebuilt 1607 under Toyotomi Hideyori & Katagiri Katsumoto * '''Nichidaijin‑sha''' (日大臣社) – sacred granary from [[Ise Grand Shrine]]; served as temporary honden 1975‑1979, then resited here * '''Tall stone lantern''' – donated by a patron linked to the [[Yodoya]] merchant house of [[Osaka]] * '''Nagahime Benzaiten‑sha''' * '''Toyomaru‑sha''' * '''Kawakami‑sha''' * '''Mikoshi‑sha''' – rebuilt 2007 (Heisei 19) * '''Shrine office''' – rebuilt September 2000 (Heisei 12)
;Off‑site * '''Otabisho''' – 19‑18 Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku, Kyoto

== Location == * 167 Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan
== Access == * 3‑minute walk (≈ 200 m SW) from [[Yodo Station]] on the [[Keihan Main Line]]
== References == * {{ill|Kurokawa Dōsuke|ja|黒川道祐|1=Kurokawa Dōyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6562509}}, ''[[Yōshū Fushi]]'' (1686) * ''Miyako Meisho Zue'' (1780) {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

Immediately south stands {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|稲葉神社}}, founded by the {{ill|Inaba clan|ja|稲葉氏|1=Inaba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975276}}, daimyō of the {{ill|Yodo Domain|ja|淀藩|1=Yodo Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844014}}, to enshrine their ancestor {{ill|Inaba Masanari|ja|稲葉正成|1=Inaba Masanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4352518}}.
== Enshrined kami == * [[Toyotama‑hime]] (also called '''Yodohime no Mikoto'''); said to be the younger sister of [[Empress Jingū]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|1=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11326733}}
== Precinct == * '''Honden''' – rebuilt March 1980 * '''Haiden''' (Important Cultural Property) – rebuilt 1607 under Toyotomi Hideyori & Katagiri Katsumoto * '''Nichidaijin‑sha''' (日大臣社) – sacred granary from [[Ise Grand Shrine]]; served as temporary honden 1975‑1979, then resited here * '''Tall stone lantern''' – donated by a patron linked to the [[Yodoya]] merchant house of [[Osaka]] * '''Nagahime Benzaiten‑sha''' * '''Toyomaru‑sha''' * '''Kawakami‑sha''' * '''Mikoshi‑sha''' – rebuilt 2007 (Heisei 19) * '''Shrine office''' – rebuilt September 2000 (Heisei 12)
;Off‑site * '''Otabisho''' – 19‑18 Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku, Kyoto

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yodo-jinja]][[ja:與杼神社]] {{infobox shinto shrine|image=Inabaj1.jpg}} '''Yodo Shrine''' (與杼神社, '''Yodo Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and under the modern shrine‑ranking system was designated a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The present grounds occupy the former main bailey of {{ill|Yodo Castle|ja|淀城|1=Yodo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559695}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the Ōwa era (961 – 964) the monk‑restorer {{ill|Chikan (Heian monk)|ja|千観|1=Senkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406805}} brought {{ill|Yodohime Myōjin|ja|淀大明神}} from {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|與止日女神社}} in Kawakami, Saga District, [[Hizen Province]] (today Ōaza Kawakami, Yamato‑chō, [[Saga]], [[Saga Prefecture]]), and founded a new shrine at Mizutare in [[Otokuni District]], [[Yamashiro Province]] (now Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku). However, the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' records that the shrine received Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in 859 (Jōgan 1), and the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' completed in 927 lists it as a small shrine of Otokuni District, indicating that it already existed before the Ōwa era.<ref name="乾杯1">[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 ‐ 京都に乾杯] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref> The shrine was revered as the guardian of river transport on the [[Katsura River]] and was also called {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|淀姫社}} or Mizutare Shrine.
In 1607 (Keichō 12) [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] ordered {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|1=Katagiri Katsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1882775}} to rebuild the honden and haiden. The honden was again reconstructed around 1649 (Keian 2).
During the [[Meiji period]] the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha. In June 1902 (Meiji 35) river‑widening works along the Katsura forced relocation to the present site on Yodo Castle’s honmaru; the former precinct became the shrine’s ''otabisho''.
On 5 August 1975 (Shōwa 50) the Important‑Cultural‑Property honden was destroyed by a fire started by middle‑school students’ fireworks, but it was rebuilt in March 1980 (Shōwa 55).
Immediately south stands {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|稲葉神社}}, founded by the {{ill|Inaba clan|ja|稲葉氏|1=Inaba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975276}}, daimyō of the {{ill|Yodo Domain|ja|淀藩|1=Yodo Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844014}}, to enshrine their ancestor {{ill|Inaba Masanari|ja|稲葉正成|1=Inaba Masanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4352518}}.
== Enshrined kami == * [[Toyotama‑hime]] (also called '''Yodohime no Mikoto'''); said to be the younger sister of [[Empress Jingū]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|1=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11326733}}
== Precinct == * '''Honden''' – rebuilt March 1980 * '''Haiden''' (Important Cultural Property) – rebuilt 1607 under Toyotomi Hideyori & Katagiri Katsumoto * '''Nichidaijin‑sha''' (日大臣社) – sacred granary from [[Ise Grand Shrine]]; served as temporary honden 1975‑1979, then resited here * '''Tall stone lantern''' – donated by a patron linked to the [[Yodoya]] merchant house of [[Osaka]] * '''Nagahime Benzaiten‑sha''' * '''Toyomaru‑sha''' * '''Kawakami‑sha''' * '''Mikoshi‑sha''' – rebuilt 2007 (Heisei 19) * '''Shrine office''' – rebuilt September 2000 (Heisei 12)
;Off‑site * '''Otabisho''' – 19‑18 Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku, Kyoto

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yodo-jinja]][[ja:與杼神社]] {{infobox shinto shrine|image=Inabaj1.jpg}} '''Yodo Shrine''' (與杼神社, '''Yodo Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and under the modern shrine‑ranking system was designated a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The present grounds occupy the former main bailey of {{ill|Yodo Castle|ja|淀城|1=Yodo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559695}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the Ōwa era (961 – 964) the monk‑restorer {{ill|Chikan (Heian monk)|ja|千観|1=Senkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406805}} brought {{ill|Yodohime Myōjin|ja|淀大明神}} from {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|與止日女神社}} in Kawakami, Saga District, [[Hizen Province]] (today Ōaza Kawakami, Yamato‑chō, [[Saga]], [[Saga Prefecture]]), and founded a new shrine at Mizutare in [[Otokuni District]], [[Yamashiro Province]] (now Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku). However, the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' records that the shrine received Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in 859 (Jōgan 1), and the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' completed in 927 lists it as a small shrine of Otokuni District, indicating that it already existed before the Ōwa era.<ref name="乾杯1">[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 ‐ 京都に乾杯] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref> The shrine was revered as the guardian of river transport on the [[Katsura River]] and was also called {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|淀姫社}} or Mizutare Shrine.
In 1607 (Keichō 12) [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] ordered {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|1=Katagiri Katsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1882775}} to rebuild the honden and haiden. The honden was again reconstructed around 1649 (Keian 2).
During the [[Meiji period]] the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha. In June 1902 (Meiji 35) river‑widening works along the Katsura forced relocation to the present site on Yodo Castle’s honmaru; the former precinct became the shrine’s ''otabisho''.
On 5 August 1975 (Shōwa 50) the Important‑Cultural‑Property honden was destroyed by a fire started by middle‑school students’ fireworks, but it was rebuilt in March 1980 (Shōwa 55).
Immediately south stands {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|稲葉神社}}, founded by the {{ill|Inaba clan|ja|稲葉氏|1=Inaba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975276}}, daimyō of the {{ill|Yodo Domain|ja|淀藩|1=Yodo Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844014}}, to enshrine their ancestor {{ill|Inaba Masanari|ja|稲葉正成|1=Inaba Masanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4352518}}.
== Enshrined kami == * [[Toyotama‑hime]] (also called '''Yodohime no Mikoto'''); said to be the younger sister of [[Empress Jingū]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|1=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11326733}}
== Precinct == * '''Honden''' – rebuilt March 1980 * '''Haiden''' (Important Cultural Property) – rebuilt 1607 under Toyotomi Hideyori & Katagiri Katsumoto * '''Nichidaijin‑sha''' (日大臣社) – sacred granary from [[Ise Grand Shrine]]; served as temporary honden 1975‑1979, then resited here * '''Tall stone lantern''' – donated by a patron linked to the [[Yodoya]] merchant house of [[Osaka]] * '''Nagahime Benzaiten‑sha''' * '''Toyomaru‑sha''' * '''Kawakami‑sha''' * '''Mikoshi‑sha''' – rebuilt 2007 (Heisei 19) * '''Shrine office''' – rebuilt September 2000 (Heisei 12)
;Off‑site * '''Otabisho''' – 19‑18 Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku, Kyoto
<gallery> File:Yodo-jinja, keidai-2.jpg|Honden & Haiden File:Inabaj2.jpg|Honden File:Yodo-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Yodo-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Auxiliary shrines File:Yodojinja01.jpg|Ginkgo tree with shimenawa File:Yodo-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii </gallery>
== Cultural properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Haiden
== Former structures == * '''Tahōtō''' – a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔}} erected (or rebuilt) during the Genroku era (1688–1704); dismantled at the start of the Meiji period during [[shinbutsu bunri]] * '''Honden''' – designated an Important Cultural Property in 1971 but lost to fire on 5 Aug 1975 when fireworks ignited its cypress‑bark roof<ref name="京都市1">[https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/shobo/page/0000159537.html 昭和50年8月5日 伏見区与杼神社本殿火災 ‐ 京都市] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref>
== Location == * 167 Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan
== Access == * 3‑minute walk (≈ 200 m SW) from [[Yodo Station]] on the [[Keihan Main Line]]
== References == * {{ill|Kurokawa Dōsuke|ja|黒川道祐|1=Kurokawa Dōyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6562509}}, ''[[Yōshū Fushi]]'' (1686) * ''Miyako Meisho Zue'' (1780) {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External links == {{Commonscat|Yodo-jinja}} {{osm box|w|388390166}} * {{official website}} * {{京都観光Navi|category_id=9|tourism_id=437|name=与杼神社}}

== Enshrined kami == * [[Toyotama‑hime]] (also called '''Yodohime no Mikoto'''); said to be the younger sister of [[Empress Jingū]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|1=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11326733}}
== Precinct == * '''Honden''' – rebuilt March 1980 * '''Haiden''' (Important Cultural Property) – rebuilt 1607 under Toyotomi Hideyori & Katagiri Katsumoto * '''Nichidaijin‑sha''' (日大臣社) – sacred granary from [[Ise Grand Shrine]]; served as temporary honden 1975‑1979, then resited here * '''Tall stone lantern''' – donated by a patron linked to the [[Yodoya]] merchant house of [[Osaka]] * '''Nagahime Benzaiten‑sha''' * '''Toyomaru‑sha''' * '''Kawakami‑sha''' * '''Mikoshi‑sha''' – rebuilt 2007 (Heisei 19) * '''Shrine office''' – rebuilt September 2000 (Heisei 12)
;Off‑site * '''Otabisho''' – 19‑18 Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku, Kyoto

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yodo-jinja]][[ja:與杼神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yodo-jinja]][[ja:與杼神社]] {{infobox shinto shrine|image=Inabaj1.jpg}} '''Yodo Shrine''' (與杼神社, '''Yodo Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and under the modern shrine‑ranking system was designated a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The present grounds occupy the former main bailey of {{ill|Yodo Castle|ja|淀城|1=Yodo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559695}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the Ōwa era (961 – 964) the monk‑restorer {{ill|Chikan (Heian monk)|ja|千観|1=Senkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406805}} brought {{ill|Yodohime Myōjin|ja|淀大明神}} from {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|與止日女神社}} in Kawakami, Saga District, [[Hizen Province]] (today Ōaza Kawakami, Yamato‑chō, [[Saga]], [[Saga Prefecture]]), and founded a new shrine at Mizutare in [[Otokuni District]], [[Yamashiro Province]] (now Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku). However, the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' records that the shrine received Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in 859 (Jōgan 1), and the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' completed in 927 lists it as a small shrine of Otokuni District, indicating that it already existed before the Ōwa era.<ref name="乾杯1">[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 ‐ 京都に乾杯] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref> The shrine was revered as the guardian of river transport on the [[Katsura River]] and was also called {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|淀姫社}} or Mizutare Shrine.
In 1607 (Keichō 12) [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] ordered {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|1=Katagiri Katsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1882775}} to rebuild the honden and haiden. The honden was again reconstructed around 1649 (Keian 2).
During the [[Meiji period]] the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha. In June 1902 (Meiji 35) river‑widening works along the Katsura forced relocation to the present site on Yodo Castle’s honmaru; the former precinct became the shrine’s ''otabisho''.
On 5 August 1975 (Shōwa 50) the Important‑Cultural‑Property honden was destroyed by a fire started by middle‑school students’ fireworks, but it was rebuilt in March 1980 (Shōwa 55).
Immediately south stands {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|稲葉神社}}, founded by the {{ill|Inaba clan|ja|稲葉氏|1=Inaba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975276}}, daimyō of the {{ill|Yodo Domain|ja|淀藩|1=Yodo Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844014}}, to enshrine their ancestor {{ill|Inaba Masanari|ja|稲葉正成|1=Inaba Masanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4352518}}.
== Enshrined kami == * [[Toyotama‑hime]] (also called '''Yodohime no Mikoto'''); said to be the younger sister of [[Empress Jingū]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|1=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11326733}}
== Precinct == * '''Honden''' – rebuilt March 1980 * '''Haiden''' (Important Cultural Property) – rebuilt 1607 under Toyotomi Hideyori & Katagiri Katsumoto * '''Nichidaijin‑sha''' (日大臣社) – sacred granary from [[Ise Grand Shrine]]; served as temporary honden 1975‑1979, then resited here * '''Tall stone lantern''' – donated by a patron linked to the [[Yodoya]] merchant house of [[Osaka]] * '''Nagahime Benzaiten‑sha''' * '''Toyomaru‑sha''' * '''Kawakami‑sha''' * '''Mikoshi‑sha''' – rebuilt 2007 (Heisei 19) * '''Shrine office''' – rebuilt September 2000 (Heisei 12)
;Off‑site * '''Otabisho''' – 19‑18 Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku, Kyoto

It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and under the modern shrine‑ranking system was designated a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The present grounds occupy the former main bailey of {{ill|Yodo Castle|ja|淀城|1=Yodo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559695}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the Ōwa era (961 – 964) the monk‑restorer {{ill|Chikan (Heian monk)|ja|千観|1=Senkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406805}} brought {{ill|Yodohime Myōjin|ja|淀大明神}} from {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|與止日女神社}} in Kawakami, Saga District, [[Hizen Province]] (today Ōaza Kawakami, Yamato‑chō, [[Saga]], [[Saga Prefecture]]), and founded a new shrine at Mizutare in [[Otokuni District]], [[Yamashiro Province]] (now Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku). However, the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' records that the shrine received Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in 859 (Jōgan 1), and the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' completed in 927 lists it as a small shrine of Otokuni District, indicating that it already existed before the Ōwa era.<ref name="乾杯1">[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 ‐ 京都に乾杯] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref> The shrine was revered as the guardian of river transport on the [[Katsura River]] and was also called {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|淀姫社}} or Mizutare Shrine.
In 1607 (Keichō 12) [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] ordered {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|1=Katagiri Katsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1882775}} to rebuild the honden and haiden. The honden was again reconstructed around 1649 (Keian 2).
During the [[Meiji period]] the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha. In June 1902 (Meiji 35) river‑widening works along the Katsura forced relocation to the present site on Yodo Castle’s honmaru; the former precinct became the shrine’s ''otabisho''.
On 5 August 1975 (Shōwa 50) the Important‑Cultural‑Property honden was destroyed by a fire started by middle‑school students’ fireworks, but it was rebuilt in March 1980 (Shōwa 55).
Immediately south stands {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|稲葉神社}}, founded by the {{ill|Inaba clan|ja|稲葉氏|1=Inaba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975276}}, daimyō of the {{ill|Yodo Domain|ja|淀藩|1=Yodo Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844014}}, to enshrine their ancestor {{ill|Inaba Masanari|ja|稲葉正成|1=Inaba Masanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4352518}}.
== Enshrined kami == * [[Toyotama‑hime]] (also called '''Yodohime no Mikoto'''); said to be the younger sister of [[Empress Jingū]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|1=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11326733}}
== Precinct == * '''Honden''' – rebuilt March 1980 * '''Haiden''' (Important Cultural Property) – rebuilt 1607 under Toyotomi Hideyori & Katagiri Katsumoto * '''Nichidaijin‑sha''' (日大臣社) – sacred granary from [[Ise Grand Shrine]]; served as temporary honden 1975‑1979, then resited here * '''Tall stone lantern''' – donated by a patron linked to the [[Yodoya]] merchant house of [[Osaka]] * '''Nagahime Benzaiten‑sha''' * '''Toyomaru‑sha''' * '''Kawakami‑sha''' * '''Mikoshi‑sha''' – rebuilt 2007 (Heisei 19) * '''Shrine office''' – rebuilt September 2000 (Heisei 12)
;Off‑site * '''Otabisho''' – 19‑18 Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku, Kyoto
<gallery> File:Yodo-jinja, keidai-2.jpg|Honden & Haiden File:Inabaj2.jpg|Honden File:Yodo-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Yodo-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Auxiliary shrines File:Yodojinja01.jpg|Ginkgo tree with shimenawa File:Yodo-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii </gallery>
== Cultural properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Haiden
== Former structures == * '''Tahōtō''' – a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔}} erected (or rebuilt) during the Genroku era (1688–1704); dismantled at the start of the Meiji period during [[shinbutsu bunri]] * '''Honden''' – designated an Important Cultural Property in 1971 but lost to fire on 5 Aug 1975 when fireworks ignited its cypress‑bark roof<ref name="京都市1">[https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/shobo/page/0000159537.html 昭和50年8月5日 伏見区与杼神社本殿火災 ‐ 京都市] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref>
== Location == * 167 Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan
== Access == * 3‑minute walk (≈ 200 m SW) from [[Yodo Station]] on the [[Keihan Main Line]]
== References == * {{ill|Kurokawa Dōsuke|ja|黒川道祐|1=Kurokawa Dōyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6562509}}, ''[[Yōshū Fushi]]'' (1686) * ''Miyako Meisho Zue'' (1780) {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External links == {{Commonscat|Yodo-jinja}} {{osm box|w|388390166}} * {{official website}} * {{京都観光Navi|category_id=9|tourism_id=437|name=与杼神社}}
{{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yodo Shrine}}

== Cultural properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Haiden
== Former structures == * '''Tahōtō''' – a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔}} erected (or rebuilt) during the Genroku era (1688–1704); dismantled at the start of the Meiji period during [[shinbutsu bunri]] * '''Honden''' – designated an Important Cultural Property in 1971 but lost to fire on 5 Aug 1975 when fireworks ignited its cypress‑bark roof<ref name="京都市1">[https://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/shobo/page/0000159537.html 昭和50年8月5日 伏見区与杼神社本殿火災 ‐ 京都市] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref>
== Location == * 167 Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan
== Access == * 3‑minute walk (≈ 200 m SW) from [[Yodo Station]] on the [[Keihan Main Line]]
== References == * {{ill|Kurokawa Dōsuke|ja|黒川道祐|1=Kurokawa Dōyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6562509}}, ''[[Yōshū Fushi]]'' (1686) * ''Miyako Meisho Zue'' (1780) {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External links == {{Commonscat|Yodo-jinja}} {{osm box|w|388390166}} * {{official website}} * {{京都観光Navi|category_id=9|tourism_id=437|name=与杼神社}}

{{wikidata link|Q11613316}} [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yodo-jinja]][[ja:與杼神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yodo-jinja]][[ja:與杼神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yodo-jinja]][[ja:與杼神社]] {{infobox shinto shrine|image=Inabaj1.jpg}} '''Yodo Shrine''' (與杼神社, '''Yodo Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in Motomachi, {{ill|Yodo, Kyoto|ja|淀|1=Yodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3572390}}, [[Fushimi‑ku]], [[Kyoto]], Japan. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and under the modern shrine‑ranking system was designated a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The present grounds occupy the former main bailey of {{ill|Yodo Castle|ja|淀城|1=Yodo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559695}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the Ōwa era (961 – 964) the monk‑restorer {{ill|Chikan (Heian monk)|ja|千観|1=Senkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406805}} brought {{ill|Yodohime Myōjin|ja|淀大明神}} from {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|與止日女神社}} in Kawakami, Saga District, [[Hizen Province]] (today Ōaza Kawakami, Yamato‑chō, [[Saga]], [[Saga Prefecture]]), and founded a new shrine at Mizutare in [[Otokuni District]], [[Yamashiro Province]] (now Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku). However, the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' records that the shrine received Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in 859 (Jōgan 1), and the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' completed in 927 lists it as a small shrine of Otokuni District, indicating that it already existed before the Ōwa era.<ref name="乾杯1">[https://www.kyotonikanpai.com/spot/04_03_toba_takeda_yodo/yodo_jinja.shtml 與杼神社 ‐ 京都に乾杯] (accessed 27 Jun 2019)</ref> The shrine was revered as the guardian of river transport on the [[Katsura River]] and was also called {{ill|Yodohime Shrine|ja|淀姫社}} or Mizutare Shrine.
In 1607 (Keichō 12) [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] ordered {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|1=Katagiri Katsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1882775}} to rebuild the honden and haiden. The honden was again reconstructed around 1649 (Keian 2).
During the [[Meiji period]] the shrine was ranked as a Gōsha. In June 1902 (Meiji 35) river‑widening works along the Katsura forced relocation to the present site on Yodo Castle’s honmaru; the former precinct became the shrine’s ''otabisho''.
On 5 August 1975 (Shōwa 50) the Important‑Cultural‑Property honden was destroyed by a fire started by middle‑school students’ fireworks, but it was rebuilt in March 1980 (Shōwa 55).
Immediately south stands {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|稲葉神社}}, founded by the {{ill|Inaba clan|ja|稲葉氏|1=Inaba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975276}}, daimyō of the {{ill|Yodo Domain|ja|淀藩|1=Yodo Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q844014}}, to enshrine their ancestor {{ill|Inaba Masanari|ja|稲葉正成|1=Inaba Masanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4352518}}.
== Enshrined kami == * [[Toyotama‑hime]] (also called '''Yodohime no Mikoto'''); said to be the younger sister of [[Empress Jingū]] * [[Takamimusubi]] * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|1=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11326733}}
== Precinct == * '''Honden''' – rebuilt March 1980 * '''Haiden''' (Important Cultural Property) – rebuilt 1607 under Toyotomi Hideyori & Katagiri Katsumoto * '''Nichidaijin‑sha''' (日大臣社) – sacred granary from [[Ise Grand Shrine]]; served as temporary honden 1975‑1979, then resited here * '''Tall stone lantern''' – donated by a patron linked to the [[Yodoya]] merchant house of [[Osaka]] * '''Nagahime Benzaiten‑sha''' * '''Toyomaru‑sha''' * '''Kawakami‑sha''' * '''Mikoshi‑sha''' – rebuilt 2007 (Heisei 19) * '''Shrine office''' – rebuilt September 2000 (Heisei 12)
;Off‑site * '''Otabisho''' – 19‑18 Yodo Oshimozu‑chō, Fushimi‑ku, Kyoto

== References == * {{ill|Kurokawa Dōsuke|ja|黒川道祐|1=Kurokawa Dōyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6562509}}, ''[[Yōshū Fushi]]'' (1686) * ''Miyako Meisho Zue'' (1780) {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
